name: The Urban Greening Plans Journey
description: Podcast providing an overview of the UGP journey
justification: Provides a useful and easy-to-understand overview of what Urban Greening Plans are and the journey cities will go on to create and deliver one

summaryBullets.user: Ideal for city officials and stakeholders new to UNPs, providing a high-level, accessible introduction.

name: URBAN GreenUP infographic
description: Infographic explaining the benefits of NBS and urban greening to raise awareness
justification: A useful tool for communicating the benefits of urban greening and UGPs to politicians and other stakeholders

summaryBullets.user: Excellent for political briefings and public-facing communication to build support for greening initiatives.

name: URBES
description: Short video to raise awareness of urban biodiversity and ecosystem services
justification: A useful tool for communicating the benefits of urban greening and UGPs to politicians and other stakeholders

summaryBullets.user: A visual and engaging tool for presentations to diverse audiences to explain the core concepts of urban ecosystems.

name: Ignition Project
description: The project aims to develop innovative financing solutions for investment in Greater Manchester’s natural environment.
justification: Demonstrates how investment in urban greening can be developed through innovative financing mechanisms

summaryBullets.user: Essential for finance departments and strategic planners in cities looking to develop sustainable, long-term funding for NBS.

name: The Nature of Cities
description: An international platform for sharing diverse, transformative ideas about cities as ecosystems of people, nature, and infrastructure.
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: A vital resource for planners, researchers, and practitioners seeking global perspectives and innovative ideas in urban ecology.

name: UNEP Generation Restoration
description: The UN Decade on Ecosystem Restoration 2021-2030, a rallying cry for the protection and revival of ecosystems all around the world
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: Connects local city efforts to a global movement, providing a strategic framework and inspiration for large-scale restoration projects.

name: The Global Platform for Sustainable Cities (GPSC)
description: GPSC is a partnership that promotes a holistic approach to sustainable urban development, fostering knowledge sharing and collaboration among cities, organizations, and experts.
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at city managers and policy makers seeking integrated solutions and best practices for sustainable urban development.

name: European Green Capital Award
description: Recognises and rewards local efforts to improve the environment, and therefore the economy and the quality of life in cities
justification: Provides a mechanism for cities to have their urban greening activities accredited at the European level

summaryBullets.user: For ambitious cities seeking to achieve high-level recognition for their environmental efforts and benchmark their performance.

name: NetworkNature
description: A one-stop-shop for all things nature-based solutions (NBS)
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: The go-to platform for anyone involved in NBS, from researchers to practitioners, offering tools, resources, and community connections.

name: ThinkNature
description: A platform that promotes the dialogue and interaction on NBS among a wide range of stakeholders
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: Focuses on stakeholder dialogue, making it particularly useful for those managing co-creation and community engagement processes.

name: Oppla
description: A knowledge marketplace, offering a wealth of resources on natural capital, ecosystem services, and nature-based solutions from a diverse range of providers.
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: A marketplace of ideas and tools, excellent for users who want to browse a wide array of solutions from various providers.

name: Urban by Nature
description: A global programme for urban nature pioneers, facilitating peer-to-peer exchange and capacity building on NBS.
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: Designed for 'urban pioneers,' this programme is great for city representatives looking for structured peer-learning and training.

name: Urban Biodiversity Hub
description: An online platform connecting a network of cities, researchers and practitioners to share knowledge and experiences in urban biodiversity conservation.
justification: Provides a wealth of information, case studies, and inspiration for cities developing UGPs

summaryBullets.user: Specifically focused on biodiversity, making it a key resource for ecologists and conservation officers in urban settings.

name: The role of public participation and stakeholder engagement in urban greening
description: Podcast providing an overview of public participation and stakeholder engagement
justification: A useful tool for communicating the benefits of urban greening and UGPs to politicians and other stakeholders

summaryBullets.user: An easy-to-digest podcast for project managers on the importance and methods of effective public engagement.

name: A Practical Guide to Creating and Managing Green Infrastructure
description: A guide for local authorities and developers in England on how to deliver green infrastructure as a key component of creating successful places.
justification: A comprehensive guide to delivering GI which is a major component of UGPs

summaryBullets.user: A go-to handbook for UK-based local authorities and developers, providing detailed, practical steps for GI delivery.

name: Green Factor Tool
description: The Green Factor Tool is a scoring system that evaluates the quality of urban greening in new developments.
justification: A tool that can be used to set urban greening targets and measure success

summaryBullets.user: Suited for urban planners and regulatory bodies needing a standardized method to assess and mandate greening in new developments.

name: i-Tree
description: A state-of-the-art, peer-reviewed software suite from the USDA Forest Service that provides urban and rural forestry analysis and benefits assessment tools.
justification: A comprehensive tool for valuing the benefits of urban trees

summaryBullets.user: A powerful tool for foresters, arborists, and planners needing to quantify the ecosystem services and economic value of trees.

name: NBS Carbon Storage Calculator
description: A simplified method for calculating the carbon storage of Nature-Based Solutions in cities
justification: A useful tool for calculating the carbon storage of NBS which can be used to justify investment

summaryBullets.user: For sustainability officers and planners needing a practical way to estimate the carbon sequestration benefits of NBS projects.

name: Naturvation Urban Nature Atlas
description: A systematically collected database of nearly 1000 examples of nature-based solutions from across 100 European cities.
justification: Provides a comprehensive database of NBS case studies from across Europe

summaryBullets.user: An essential starting point for planners and researchers looking for real-world examples and inspiration for NBS interventions.

name: Green pass
description: A certification and optimization tool for climate-resilient urban planning and architecture
justification: A holistic tool for assessing the climate performance of urban development projects

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at architects, developers, and planners seeking a robust certification for climate-resilient design.

name: URBAN GreenUP NBS catalogue
description: A catalogue of different types of NBS with information on their characteristics, benefits, and implementation considerations
justification: Provides a comprehensive database of NBS case studies from across Europe

summaryBullets.user: A practical catalogue for users who need to understand the specifics of different NBS types to select the right ones for their project.

name: BioClim
description: Software to analyze the microclimate effects of vegetation in urban areas
justification: Tool for detailed microclimate analysis

summaryBullets.user: Suits architects and urban climate specialists needing detailed simulations of vegetation's cooling effects.

name: CityTree
description: Moss-based air filter to combat air pollution
justification: Innovative technology for air quality improvement

summaryBullets.user: For city managers targeting air pollution hotspots with a high-tech, green infrastructure solution.

name: TOC (Trees, Water & People)
description: Community-based natural resource management and renewable energy solutions
justification: Example of a strong community-based programme

summaryBullets.user: A model for NGOs and community organizers aiming to implement grassroots environmental projects.

name: Drought-Tolerant Landscaping Guide
description: A guide to creating water-wise gardens and landscapes
justification: Practical guidance for water conservation

summaryBullets.user: Essential for landscape designers and homeowners in water-scarce regions.

name: ESRI ArcGIS Urban
description: A 3D experience for urban planning that allows you to visualize projects in your city and see their impacts.
justification: Powerful GIS tool for integrated urban planning

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at professional urban planning departments requiring advanced 3D visualization and impact assessment capabilities.

name: Nature Smart Cities
description: Interreg project to help cities develop and implement green infrastructure action plans.
justification: Provides a business model and decision support tool for GI.

summaryBullets.user: Suits city administrators needing to build a strong economic case for green infrastructure investments.

name: INaturalist
description: A citizen science project and online social network of naturalists, citizen scientists, and biologists built on the concept of mapping and sharing observations of biodiversity across the globe.
justification: Excellent tool for citizen science and biodiversity monitoring.

summaryBullets.user: Perfect for engaging the public, schools, and community groups in local biodiversity mapping efforts.

name: WWF Biodiversity Risk Filter
description: A web-based tool that helps companies and financial institutions assess and respond to biodiversity-related risks.
justification: Tool for assessing biodiversity risks in supply chains.

summaryBullets.user: Designed for corporate sustainability managers and financial institutions to evaluate biodiversity impacts.

name: EKLIPSE
description: A European mechanism for providing evidence-based knowledge on biodiversity and ecosystem services to policymakers.
justification: Connects policymakers with scientific expertise.

summaryBullets.user: For policymakers and their advisors needing robust, synthesized scientific knowledge on biodiversity issues.

name: Green Roof Manual
description: A comprehensive guide to the benefits, design, installation, and maintenance of green roofs.
justification: Detailed technical guidance for green roof implementation.

summaryBullets.user: An essential technical guide for architects, engineers, and contractors involved in green roof projects.

name: Miro
description: An online collaborative whiteboard platform that enables distributed teams to work together effectively.
justification: Excellent tool for remote workshops and stakeholder engagement.

summaryBullets.user: A versatile tool for facilitators and project managers running virtual or hybrid collaborative sessions.

name: CitizenLab
description: A digital democracy platform for local governments to engage their communities and manage their participation projects.
justification: Comprehensive platform for digital citizen participation.

summaryBullets.user: Designed for local government departments seeking to manage and scale their citizen engagement efforts online.

name: Public Sector Decarbonisation Guidance
description: Guidance for UK public sector bodies on how to decarbonise their estates.
justification: Provides a framework for linking greening with decarbonisation goals.

summaryBullets.user: Specifically for UK public sector estate managers working on decarbonisation plans.

name: Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures (TNFD)
description: A global market-led initiative to develop a risk management and disclosure framework for organizations to report and act on evolving nature-related risks.
justification: Provides a corporate framework for nature-related financial disclosure.

summaryBullets.user: Essential for large corporations and financial institutions needing to align with emerging global standards on nature risk.

name: Greenkeeper
description: App for managing golf course maintenance, which can be adapted for park management.
justification: Tool for optimizing maintenance of green spaces.

summaryBullets.user: Suits park and grounds managers looking for a digital solution to schedule and track maintenance tasks.

name: OpenStreetMap
description: A collaborative project to create a free editable map of the world.
justification: Provides free, open-source geospatial data.

summaryBullets.user: A foundational data source for GIS analysts, community mappers, and anyone needing free, customizable map data.

name: Local Authority Pollinator Toolkit
description: A toolkit for local authorities in the UK to help pollinators.
justification: Specific guidance for a key biodiversity group.

summaryBullets.user: A practical guide for UK local authority ecologists and park managers on creating pollinator-friendly habitats.

name: GIO-TRAFx
description: Automated counters and data services for pedestrian and bicycle traffic.
justification: Tool for monitoring the use of greenways and parks.

summaryBullets.user: For park managers and transport planners needing accurate data on pedestrian and cyclist numbers.

name: UN Decade on Ecosystem Restoration - Monitoring Framework
description: A framework to track progress of the UN Decade, providing standards and tools for monitoring ecosystem restoration.
justification: Global framework for monitoring restoration efforts.

summaryBullets.user: For national and local governments aligning their restoration projects with global reporting standards.

name: Biodiversity Intactness Index (BII)
description: An indicator that estimates how much of a region’s natural biodiversity still persists.
justification: A high-level indicator for assessing biodiversity status.

summaryBullets.user: Suits national and regional environmental agencies for high-level biodiversity reporting and trend analysis.

name: UNaLab
description: Urban Nature Labs - an H2020 project demonstrating innovative, co-created nature-based solutions in cities.
justification: Provides real-world case studies and a co-creation framework.

summaryBullets.user: For city planners interested in the practical application and co-creation process of NBS demonstration projects.

name: BiodiverCities by 2030
description: A World Economic Forum initiative to help cities integrate nature as a core part of their development.
justification: High-level initiative promoting the integration of nature into urban development.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at city leaders and high-level decision-makers to build the case for nature-positive urban futures.

name: European Platform for Urban Greening
description: Platform to bring together expertise in urban greening from across Europe, focusing on skills and knowledge for professionals.
justification: Vocational training and skills-focused platform.

summaryBullets.user: For professionals and students in landscaping and green space management seeking to upgrade their skills.

name: The IUCN Global Standard for Nature-based Solutions
description: A framework for the verification, design, and scaling up of Nature-based Solutions.
justification: A globally recognized standard to ensure the quality and effectiveness of NBS.

summaryBullets.user: Essential for project developers, investors, and policymakers needing to design and verify high-quality NBS.

name: GrowGreen
description: H2020 project demonstrating NBS in several cities and developing strategies for upscaling.
justification: Provides case studies and strategies for NBS implementation and financing.

summaryBullets.user: For cities looking for proven examples of NBS and guidance on how to replicate and scale them up.

name: CIBSE AM14: Non-domestic cooling
description: Technical guidance on cooling buildings, including the role of green infrastructure.
justification: Engineering guidance on how GI contributes to building cooling.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at mechanical engineers and architects designing cooling systems for non-domestic buildings.

name: CLEARING HOUSE
description: A research project focusing on urban forests as nature-based solutions (UF-NBS).
justification: Provides in-depth knowledge and tools related to urban forests.

summaryBullets.user: For researchers, foresters, and planners specializing in the management and benefits of urban forests.

name: QGIS
description: A free and open-source geographic information system (GIS).
justification: A powerful, free alternative to commercial GIS software for spatial analysis.

summaryBullets.user: The essential tool for anyone needing to perform geospatial analysis without a budget for commercial software.

name: Climate-ADAPT
description: The European platform for climate adaptation knowledge.
justification: A central repository of information on climate change adaptation in Europe.

summaryBullets.user: A key resource for policymakers and practitioners across Europe working on climate adaptation strategies.

name: GoGreenRoutes
description: An H2020 project exploring the links between urban nature, health, and well-being.
justification: Focuses on the health and well-being co-benefits of urban greening.

summaryBullets.user: For public health officials and urban planners interested in the evidence base linking green space and human health.

name: Green Surge
description: An FP7 project that identified, developed, and tested ways of linking green spaces, biodiversity, and sustainability.
justification: Provides foundational research and guidance on urban green infrastructure planning.

summaryBullets.user: Offers foundational research and frameworks for planners developing strategic green infrastructure plans.

name: EU Pollinators Initiative
description: The European Commission's initiative to address the decline of pollinators.
justification: Sets the policy context for local action on pollinators.

summaryBullets.user: For national and local policymakers needing to understand and contribute to the EU's strategy on pollinators.

name: Global Mangrove Watch
description: An online platform that provides the remote sensing data and tools for monitoring the world’s mangroves.
justification: Specialized tool for a key coastal ecosystem.

summaryBullets.user: A crucial tool for coastal managers and conservationists in tropical and subtropical regions.

name: Building with Nature
description: A UK-based benchmark and accreditation system for high-quality green infrastructure.
justification: Provides a standard for quality GI in new developments.

summaryBullets.user: For UK developers, planners, and local authorities seeking a recognized standard for excellent green infrastructure.

name: Natural Capital Planning Tool (NCPT)
description: A free, web-based tool for local authorities in England to assess the natural capital impacts of planning proposals.
justification: Helps integrate natural capital assessment into planning decisions.

summaryBullets.user: Designed for planning officers in England to help evaluate the environmental impacts of development plans.

name: Conexkt
description: A platform for mapping stakeholders and visualizing their relationships.
justification: Useful for understanding and managing complex stakeholder networks.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at project managers and community organizers who need to analyze and navigate complex stakeholder landscapes.

name: BREEAM
description: The world's leading sustainability assessment method for masterplanning projects, infrastructure and buildings.
justification: A comprehensive, internationally recognized standard for sustainable buildings and communities.

summaryBullets.user: For developers, architects, and clients seeking a globally recognized certification for building sustainability.

name: ENCORE
description: A tool to help financial institutions understand and integrate natural capital risks and opportunities into their activities.
justification: Connects environmental change with its economic consequences.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at the finance industry to help analysts and portfolio managers assess nature-related risks.

name: REGREEN
description: An H2020 project focusing on fostering urban liveability through nature-based solutions.
justification: Provides tools and insights for co-creating NBS for liveable cities.

summaryBullets.user: For city planners and community groups focused on improving urban liveability through co-created nature-based solutions.

name: The Dasgupta Review
description: An independent, global review on the Economics of Biodiversity led by Professor Sir Partha Dasgupta.
justification: A foundational document making the economic case for biodiversity.

summaryBullets.user: Essential reading for policymakers, economists, and leaders to understand the economic imperative of protecting nature.

name: Local Green Space designation
description: A UK policy tool that allows communities to nominate local green areas for special protection.
justification: A mechanism for community-led protection of green spaces.

summaryBullets.user: For community groups and parish councils in the UK wanting to legally protect valued local green spaces.

name: Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF)
description: An international network and data infrastructure funded by the world's governments and aimed at providing anyone, anywhere, open access to data about all types of life on Earth.
justification: The largest source of open-access biodiversity data.

summaryBullets.user: An indispensable resource for researchers, conservationists, and policymakers needing access to global biodiversity data.

name: Commonplace
description: An online platform for community engagement and consultation, focused on placemaking.
justification: A user-friendly platform for gathering community feedback on development projects.

summaryBullets.user: For developers and local authorities who need to run effective, map-based consultations for planning and regeneration projects.

name: Perfect Pollinator Plants
description: Guidance from the Royal Horticultural Society on which plants to choose to support pollinators.
justification: A practical, evidence-based plant list for supporting pollinators.

summaryBullets.user: A go-to guide for gardeners, landscape designers, and park managers wanting to create pollinator-friendly planting schemes.

name: Living Roofs and Walls Technical Report
description: A technical report supporting the London Plan policy on living roofs and walls.
justification: Detailed technical support for implementing green roofs and walls.

summaryBullets.user: Specifically for those working on projects in London, providing the technical detail needed to comply with the London Plan.

name: Integrated Valuation of Ecosystem Services and Trade-offs (InVEST)
description: A suite of free, open-source software models used to map and value the goods and services from nature that sustain and fulfill human life.
justification: A powerful tool for modeling and valuing ecosystem services.

summaryBullets.user: For GIS-savvy environmental planners and researchers needing to model and quantify ecosystem service trade-offs.

name: Urban Tree Manual
description: A manual providing advice on selecting and procuring urban trees.
justification: Practical guidance on tree selection for urban environments.

summaryBullets.user: An essential guide for landscape architects, urban foresters, and procurement officers involved in urban tree planting.

name: GPlates
description: Open-source software for the interactive visualisation of plate-tectonics.
justification: Highly specialized tool for deep-time earth science, not directly relevant to UNPs but an example of open-source science tools.

summaryBullets.user: Exclusively for geologists and earth scientists studying plate tectonics.

name: Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) tools
description: Tools and methodologies for assessing the environmental impacts associated with all the stages of a product's life.
justification: Can be used to assess the environmental footprint of green infrastructure projects.

summaryBullets.user: For sustainability professionals and engineers conducting detailed life cycle assessments of products or projects.

name: Water-Sensitive Urban Design (WSUD)
description: A holistic approach to urban water management that integrates the water cycle into urban design.
justification: A comprehensive framework for sustainable urban water management.

summaryBullets.user: A key framework for water engineers, urban planners, and landscape architects designing integrated water systems.

name: Survey123 for ArcGIS
description: A form-centric solution for creating, sharing and analyzing surveys.
justification: A powerful tool for creating smart surveys for field data collection.

summaryBullets.user: For organizations using the ESRI ecosystem to conduct field surveys and data collection.

name: SWOT Analysis Tool
description: A strategic planning technique used to help a person or organization identify strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats related to business competition or project planning.
justification: A simple and effective framework for strategic analysis.

summaryBullets.user: A universally applicable tool for any team or individual at the start of a strategic planning process.

name: Nature Conservancy's Carbon Planner
description: A tool to identify areas where nature-based solutions can have the biggest impact on climate mitigation.
justification: Helps prioritize investments in natural climate solutions.

summaryBullets.user: For conservation planners and policymakers looking to identify high-impact areas for natural climate solutions.

name: Google Earth Engine
description: A platform for petabyte-scale scientific analysis and visualization of geospatial datasets.
justification: A powerful cloud-based platform for large-scale environmental monitoring.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at researchers and data scientists with coding skills needing to process massive geospatial datasets.

name: Healthy Urbanism
description: A framework and tool from BRE for assessing and improving the health and wellbeing impacts of urban development.
justification: Integrates health and wellbeing into the core of urban planning.

summaryBullets.user: For planners, developers, and public health professionals seeking to create healthier urban environments.

name: EU Green Deal
description: The EU's overarching strategy to make Europe the first climate-neutral continent.
justification: The highest-level policy driver for all urban greening and sustainability efforts in the EU.

summaryBullets.user: Essential context for all policymakers and public bodies operating within the European Union.

name: CitiesWithNature
description: A global platform for cities to connect, share, and learn about mainstreaming nature into their planning and development.
justification: A key network for cities committed to working with nature.

summaryBullets.user: For city representatives and officials wanting to join a global community of practice on urban nature.

name: Sustainable Urban Drainage Systems (SUDS) Manual
description: The most comprehensive and up-to-date guidance on the planning, design, construction, and maintenance of Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS) in the UK.
justification: The definitive technical guide for SuDS implementation.

summaryBullets.user: The essential reference for civil engineers, landscape architects, and developers implementing SuDS in the UK.

name: MAES (Mapping and Assessment of Ecosystems and their Services)
description: An initiative under the EU Biodiversity Strategy to map and assess the state of ecosystems and their services in the EU.
justification: Provides the methodological framework for national ecosystem assessments.

summaryBullets.user: Primarily for national and regional government bodies responsible for conducting ecosystem assessments.

name: Forest Europe
description: The pan-European voluntary high-level political process for the protection and sustainable management of the continent’s forests.
justification: High-level policy process for forests, relevant to urban forestry strategy.

summaryBullets.user: For national forestry departments and policymakers involved in pan-European forest strategy.

name: Biodiversity Net Gain
description: A UK policy that requires developers to ensure they leave biodiversity in a better state than before.
justification: A mandatory policy tool in the UK for quantifying and delivering biodiversity improvements.

summaryBullets.user: Mandatory for most developers and local planning authorities in England to understand and implement.

name: LIFE Programme
description: The EU's funding instrument for the environment and climate action.
justification: A major source of funding for environmental and nature conservation projects.

summaryBullets.user: For any organization (public, private, NGO) in the EU seeking funding for environmental, nature conservation, and climate action projects.

name: DGNB System
description: A German certification system for sustainable building.
justification: A comprehensive sustainability certification system with a strong focus in Germany.

summaryBullets.user: Primarily for architects, developers, and clients in Germany and Europe seeking a holistic sustainability certification.

name: Horizon Europe
description: The EU's key funding programme for research and innovation.
justification: The primary source of EU funding for R&I projects, including many related to urban greening and NBS.

summaryBullets.user: For universities, research institutes, businesses, and other organizations involved in cutting-edge research and innovation.

name: Global Covenant of Mayors for Climate & Energy
description: The largest global alliance for city climate leadership, built upon the commitment of over 10,000 cities and local governments.
justification: A major global network for cities committed to climate action.

summaryBullets.user: For mayors and city administrations wanting to commit to climate action and join a global network for support and reporting.

name: Urban Innovation Actions (UIA)
description: An EU initiative that provides urban areas throughout Europe with resources to test new and unproven solutions to address urban challenges.
justification: Funding for innovative and experimental urban projects.

summaryBullets.user: For ambitious city authorities wanting to pilot bold and innovative solutions to their most pressing challenges.

name: Landscape Institute's Digital Tools for Landscape
description: A report reviewing the digital tools available for landscape professionals.
justification: A useful overview of the digital technology landscape for the profession.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at landscape architects and related professionals looking to understand and adopt new digital tools.

name: URBACT
description: A European exchange and learning programme promoting sustainable urban development, co-financed by the EU.
justification: Fosters peer-learning and knowledge exchange between European cities.

summaryBullets.user: For city practitioners wanting to learn from and solve problems with peers from other European cities.

name: Interreg Europe
description: An EU-funded programme that helps regional and local governments across Europe to develop and deliver better policy.
justification: Funds projects for interregional cooperation and policy learning.

summaryBullets.user: For public authorities seeking to improve their regional development policies through cooperation with partners in other regions.

name: EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030
description: A comprehensive, ambitious, long-term plan to protect nature and reverse the degradation of ecosystems.
justification: The key EU policy document driving biodiversity action, including the requirement for Urban Greening Plans.

summaryBullets.user: Essential context for all policymakers and public bodies, as it sets the specific targets that UNPs must help deliver.

name: COST Actions
description: Funding for research and innovation networks.
justification: Supports networking among researchers, enabling them to collaborate on topics of their choice.

summaryBullets.user: Primarily for researchers and innovators looking to build or join collaborative networks across Europe and beyond.

name: The Place Standard Tool
description: A tool to support the delivery of high-quality places in Scotland, providing a framework to structure conversations about place.
justification: A simple and effective tool for assessing place quality and facilitating community discussions.

summaryBullets.user: A great tool for communities, planners, and public bodies in Scotland (and adaptable elsewhere) to discuss and evaluate their local environment.

name: JPI Urban Europe
description: A research and innovation hub for urban transitions.
justification: Coordinates research and innovation on urban challenges at a European level.

summaryBullets.user: For researchers, cities, and businesses involved in addressing complex urban challenges through R&I.

name: EIT Climate-KIC
description: Europe’s leading climate innovation agency and community.
justification: Supports systemic change and innovation for climate action.

summaryBullets.user: For innovators, entrepreneurs, and city leaders looking for support, funding, and collaboration to drive climate action.

name: C40 Cities
description: A network of mayors of nearly 100 world-leading cities collaborating to confront the climate crisis.
justification: A leading global network for city climate leadership, focused on large metropolises.

summaryBullets.user: For mayoral offices and senior administrators in the world's largest cities committed to ambitious climate action.

name: ICLEI - Local Governments for Sustainability
description: A global network of more than 2500 local and regional governments committed to sustainable urban development.
justification: A major global network supporting cities of all sizes on their sustainability journey.

summaryBullets.user: For local and regional governments of all sizes seeking guidance, tools, and advocacy support on sustainability.

name: Eurocities
description: The network of major European cities.
justification: A key network for large European cities to share knowledge and influence EU policy.

summaryBullets.user: For the administrations of major European cities looking to collaborate and engage with EU institutions.

name: Urban Agenda for the EU
description: A multi-level governance method to promote cooperation between Member States, cities, the European Commission and other stakeholders.
justification: A collaborative process to improve EU legislation, funding, and knowledge for cities.

summaryBullets.user: For policymakers at the city, national, and EU level involved in shaping the future of urban development policy.

name: ESPON
description: The European Territorial Observation Network, providing pan-European territorial data, analysis and maps.
justification: A key source of data and evidence on European territorial trends.

summaryBullets.user: For policymakers and researchers needing comparative data and analysis across European regions and cities.

name: Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC)
description: A global consortium developing and promoting open standards for geospatial data and services.
justification: Drives the interoperability of geospatial data, which is fundamental to many planning tools.

summaryBullets.user: For software developers, data providers, and large organizations working with geospatial technology.

name: INSPIRE Geoportal
description: The central European access point to spatial data from EU Member States under the INSPIRE directive.
justification: A key data infrastructure for harmonized spatial data across Europe.

summaryBullets.user: For public authorities and researchers needing access to standardized, trans-boundary environmental spatial data in the EU.

name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service
description: Provides geographical information on land cover and on variables related, for instance, to the vegetation state, the water cycle or the earth's surface energy balance.
justification: A major source of free, satellite-based land cover and environmental data for Europe.

summaryBullets.user: An essential resource for environmental agencies, land use planners, and researchers needing authoritative land data for Europe.

name: GEOSS Portal
description: A single internet access point for Earth observation data from all over the world.
justification: A global portal to a wide range of Earth observation data.

summaryBullets.user: For scientists and researchers needing to discover and access a diverse range of Earth observation datasets from global providers.

name: ArcGIS StoryMaps
description: A tool to create inspiring, immersive stories by combining maps with narrative text, images, and multimedia content.
justification: A powerful tool for communicating plans and project outcomes.

summaryBullets.user: For communicators, educators, and planners wanting to create compelling, map-based narratives for a public audience.

name: Tableau Public
description: A free platform to explore, create and publicly share data visualizations.
justification: A popular tool for creating interactive charts and dashboards.

summaryBullets.user: For data analysts, journalists, and anyone wanting to create and share rich, interactive data visualizations online.

name: Canva
description: An online design and publishing tool.
justification: An easy-to-use tool for creating professional-looking graphics, presentations, and reports.

summaryBullets.user: Accessible to everyone, from non-designers to professionals, for creating a wide range of visual content.

name: Engaged Journalism Accelerator
description: An initiative to support and champion engaged journalism in European newsrooms.
justification: Provides models and inspiration for community engagement and communication.

summaryBullets.user: For journalists, communicators, and community managers looking for best practices in community engagement.

name: Nature-based Solutions Initiative
description: An interdisciplinary programme of research, education and policy advice based at the University of Oxford.
justification: A leading academic hub for NBS, providing evidence, policy advice, and educational resources.

summaryBullets.user: Aimed at policymakers, practitioners, and researchers seeking robust scientific evidence and analysis on NBS.

name: City-Business Climate Alliance (CBCA)
description: An alliance that brings together city and business leaders to accelerate climate action.
justification: A model for public-private collaboration on climate goals.

summaryBullets.user: For city governments and and businesses looking to form local partnerships to deliver on climate commitments.

name: Green City Accord
description: A movement of European mayors committed to making cities greener, cleaner and healthier.
justification: A commitment framework for European cities on a range of environmental targets.

summaryBullets.user: For European mayors and city councils wanting to commit to and report on ambitious environmental targets.

name: TCPA Garden City Principles
description: A set of principles for creating new, sustainable communities.
justification: A foundational framework for new community planning that integrates green space and social value.

summaryBullets.user: For planners, developers, and local authorities involved in the masterplanning of new towns and large-scale developments.

name: Partnerships for Forests
description: An incubator programme that supports and develops forest-positive businesses and partnerships.
justification: A model for financing sustainable forestry and agricultural commodity projects.

summaryBullets.user: For entrepreneurs and businesses with innovative ideas for protecting and managing forests sustainably.

name: NBS EduWORLD
description: A project to create a comprehensive NBS education ecosystem.
justification: Developing educational resources and curricula for NBS.

summaryBullets.user: Primarily for educators, trainers, and academic institutions looking to develop or integrate NBS into their teaching.

name: European Topic Centre on Urban, Land and Soil systems (ETC-ULS)
description: A centre of expertise that assists the European Environment Agency (EEA) with data and analysis on urban issues.
justification: Provides the evidence base for the EEA's work on urban environments.

summaryBullets.user: For those needing the detailed data and reports that underpin the European Environment Agency's assessments.

name: Google Environmental Insights Explorer
description: Provides data and insights on carbon emissions and renewable energy potential for thousands of cities.
justification: An accessible source of data for city-level climate action planning.

summaryBullets.user: For city staff, researchers, and advocates needing easily accessible data on emissions and solar potential.

name: Bat Conservation Trust - Encouraging bats
description: Guidance on how to make gardens and green spaces more friendly for bats.
justification: Practical advice for supporting a specific, protected group of species.

summaryBullets.user: A simple guide for gardeners, homeowners, and park managers on how to support local bat populations.

name: World Bank Open Data
description: Free and open access to global development data.
justification: A vast repository of socioeconomic and development data.

summaryBullets.user: An essential resource for economists, researchers, and policymakers working on development issues.

name: SITES (Sweden's Infrastructure for Ecosystem Science)
description: A national research infrastructure for terrestrial and limnological field research.
justification: A model for long-term ecological monitoring infrastructure.

summaryBullets.user: Primarily for ecologists and environmental scientists needing access to long-term monitoring data and research sites in Sweden.

name: Open Air Laboratories (OPAL)
description: A UK-wide citizen science initiative that allows you to get hands-on with nature, whatever your age, background or ability.
justification: A highly successful model for large-scale citizen science and environmental education.

summaryBullets.user: A great resource for educators, community groups, and families wanting to participate in national environmental surveys.

name: Global Adaptation Mapping Initiative (GAMI)
description: An initiative to systematically map the landscape of climate change adaptation research.
justification: Provides a systematic review of adaptation literature.

summaryBullets.user: For academics and policy analysts needing a systematic overview of the state of adaptation research.

name: Healthy Streets
description: A framework of 10 evidence-based indicators for creating healthier, more inclusive streets.
justification: A human-centred framework for street design.

summaryBullets.user: For transport planners, urban designers, and public health practitioners looking to create people-friendly streets.

name: UN-Habitat
description: The United Nations programme for human settlements and sustainable urban development.
justification: A key global agency setting the agenda for sustainable urbanization.

summaryBullets.user: For national and local governments, and development professionals seeking alignment with global urban development goals.

name: TCPA Guide to Community-led housing
description: A guide for communities on how to develop their own housing projects.
justification: Empowers communities to take the lead in their own development.

summaryBullets.user: For community groups and individuals interested in exploring and developing community-led housing schemes.

name: Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the UN
description: A specialized agency of the United Nations that leads international efforts to defeat hunger and improve nutrition and food security.
justification: A key global agency for food systems, with growing work on urban forestry and agriculture.

summaryBullets.user: For policymakers and practitioners working on food systems, from rural agriculture to urban farming.

name: Global Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction
description: The main global forum to assess and discuss progress on the implementation of the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction.
justification: A key global platform for DRR, where the role of ecosystem-based solutions is increasingly important.

summaryBullets.user: For national disaster management agencies and policymakers involved in DRR strategy and implementation.
